disclaimer: Curated high-confidence cascade edges (F6/F8). Propose-only watchlist — never invents proven class membership. Ordinal bands are shop priors, not actuarial probabilities. Engine-family filters gate OEM-specific consequents. manualCondition antecedents require operator-entered stages (never from OBD alone).

edges.rationale: Chronic misfire dumps raw fuel into the catalyst and commonly cooks the brick before a P0420 appears.
edges.horizon: Often within a few hundred miles of continued misfire under load

edges.rationale: Sustained lean / high positive trim stresses catalyst light-off and oxygen storage.
edges.horizon: Miles to short seasons depending on severity

edges.rationale: A rising LTFT trend is early lean evidence — watch for a bank-1 lean code and intake/fuel issues.
edges.horizon: Next several drive cycles if the trend continues

edges.rationale: Upstream O2 performance faults corrupt fuel control; catalyst efficiency often follows if ignored.
edges.horizon: Variable — fix the sensor path before chasing the cat

edges.rationale: Timing correlation / CKP-CMP faults commonly present next as misfire under load if the engine keeps running.
edges.horizon: Immediate to short-term if driven

edges.rationale: Tigershark MultiAir is oil-actuated — chronic oil loss is the classic path into oil-starvation actuator damage (TSB family).
edges.horizon: Soon if oil level/quality stays neglected

edges.rationale: An open misfire case that has not been verified closed still implies catalyst risk while the vehicle is driven.
edges.horizon: While the case remains open and driven

edges.rationale: Incorrect / high purge flow dumps vapor into the intake and commonly drives positive fuel trim / lean codes.
edges.horizon: Next several drive cycles if purge stays stuck open

edges.rationale: Thin pads leave less margin — continued driving commonly scores rotors before the next pad change.
edges.horizon: Soon if driven hard or pads are near minimum

edges.rationale: Metal-on-metal pad wear machines the rotor face; expect rotor replacement with the pads.
edges.horizon: Immediate — do not keep driving on grinding brakes

edges.rationale: Severely scored / heat-damaged rotors and hard stops stress hub assemblies; watch for growl / play next.
edges.horizon: Next seasons if vibration and heat keep cycling the hub

edges.rationale: Dark / overdue fluid usually means moisture — seals and ABS hardware corrode next if ignored.
edges.horizon: Seasons if fluid stays neglected

edges.rationale: A sticking caliper forces tapered pad wear on that corner and often the opposite side.
edges.horizon: Immediate to short-term while driven

edges.rationale: A seized caliper drags and heat-spots the rotor — replace or machine with the caliper repair.
edges.horizon: Immediate if the wheel is running hot

edges.rationale: Load-sensitive hub growl commonly progresses to play, ABS tone-ring damage, or hub seizure.
edges.horizon: Soon — do not ignore a growing growl

edges.rationale: Outer CV click on lock-steer usually means a torn boot / dry joint heading for axle replacement.
edges.horizon: Miles to short seasons depending on severity

edges.rationale: A cracked belt can shed ribs and strand the alternator / water pump / power steering without warning.
edges.horizon: Soon if cracks are deep or chunks missing

edges.rationale: Soft/swollen hoses commonly split at the end of a heat cycle — watch for coolant smell and level loss.
edges.horizon: Next seasons or next hard heat soak

edges.rationale: Coil circuit faults commonly present next as cylinder misfire under load if driven.
edges.horizon: Immediate to short-term if driven

edges.rationale: An open/shorted injector circuit commonly cascades into a hard misfire on that cylinder.
edges.horizon: Immediate if the circuit stays open
